requirements: Frontend and backend MUST use compatible protocols
Message flow MUST be testable end-to-end
Protocol boundaries MUST be explicitly validated
Communication layers MUST be independently testable

architecturalConstraints: Client and server MUST use same WebSocket library family
Protocol negotiation MUST be explicit and tested
Message format MUST be validated at boundaries
Transport failures MUST be distinguishable from protocol failures

testFirst.assertion: Socket.IO client connects to Socket.IO server OR raw WebSocket to raw WebSocket

testFirst.implementation: Serialize message on client, deserialize on server
Send input from frontend, verify PTY receives it
testFirst.assertion: Input reaches backend process without protocol translation
Business logic independent of transport protocol

protocolCompatibilityTest.description: Validates that frontend and backend use compatible protocols
protocolCompatibilityTest.prevents: Socket.IO client connecting to raw WebSocket server

endToEndFlowTest.description: Validates complete message flow from user input to backend processing
endToEndFlowTest.prevents: Silent communication failures where input never reaches backend

messageIntegrityTest.description: Validates message format consistency across protocol boundaries
messageIntegrityTest.prevents: Message corruption or format changes during transmission

beforeImplementation: [ ] Define exact protocol requirements (Socket.IO vs raw WebSocket)
[ ] All tests MUST FAIL initially (red phase)
duringImplementation: [ ] Implement minimal code to pass protocol compatibility test
[ ] Implement minimal code to pass end-to-end test
[ ] Implement minimal code to pass message integrity test
beforeDeployment: [ ] No protocol assumptions in code comments

earlyWarnings: Frontend logs show 'connected' but no backend message logs
Backend shows connections but no message handler invocations
Transport layer successful but application layer silent
Protocol mismatch errors in browser dev tools network tab
